notes: One of the most well-known apprenticeship programs in tech. You get a full-time salary, benefits, mentorship from Google engineers, and structured training. Many apprentices convert to full-time Googlers. Highly competitive — strong portfolio and clear career narrative help you stand out.
requirements: No degree required. Must demonstrate relevant skills through portfolio, certifications, or equivalent experience.

notes: A 16-week immersive program designed for career changers. You'll work on real Microsoft teams with real responsibilities. Cohorts run in Redmond, Atlanta, and international locations. Strong conversion rate to full-time Microsoft roles after the program.
requirements: Career changers from non-traditional backgrounds. Some technical foundation required (bootcamp, self-taught, or certificate).
location: Redmond, WA | Atlanta, GA | International cohorts

notes: One of the strongest apprenticeship programs for software engineers. It's a multi-year program that starts with intensive training and gradually integrates you into a full engineering role. You get a real salary, stock options, and mentorship. LinkedIn is known for its supportive engineering culture.
requirements: Non-traditional background in software engineering. Bootcamp grads, self-taught developers, and career changers encouraged to apply.

notes: A 6-month apprenticeship specifically designed for engineers from non-traditional backgrounds. You'll work alongside senior Airbnb engineers on real production code. Airbnb is known for their strong engineering culture and beautiful product. Great conversion rates to full-time.
requirements: Must be from a non-traditional background (no CS degree required). Should have foundational coding skills.

notes: Focuses on bringing engineers from non-traditional backgrounds into Pinterest's engineering team. You'll work on real products used by millions. Pinterest has a collaborative, supportive culture that prioritizes growth and learning.
requirements: Non-traditional background. Foundational coding skills from bootcamp, self-study, or community college.

notes: Work at the frontier of AI safety. Anthropic's residency program is research and engineering focused, placing you on teams building Claude and other AI systems. Ideal for people transitioning into AI/ML. You'll learn from world-class researchers.
requirements: Strong technical foundation. Interest in AI safety and alignment. Some programming experience required.

notes: Targeted at self-taught developers and bootcamp graduates. You'll work on real ride-sharing infrastructure with experienced mentors. Lyft values diverse perspectives and non-traditional paths into engineering.
requirements: Self-taught or bootcamp background. Foundational coding skills in Python or JavaScript.
location: San Francisco, CA | New York, NY

notes: Engineering apprenticeship for non-traditional backgrounds. Program availability may vary — check the careers page for current openings. When available, it offers hands-on experience with large-scale distributed systems.
requirements: Varies by cohort. Generally requires foundational programming skills.

notes: IBM was one of the first major tech companies to launch an apprenticeship program. They hire for 'new collar' roles — positions that require skills, not necessarily degrees. You get paid training, mentorship, and a real job with benefits. Multiple tracks available.
requirements: No degree required. Must complete pre-apprenticeship coursework (often provided free by IBM partners).

notes: A free training program that prepares you for roles in the Salesforce ecosystem. You learn Salesforce platform skills and get placed into jobs at Salesforce partner companies. The Salesforce ecosystem has over 9 million jobs — this is a huge opportunity.
requirements: No experience required. Must complete application and interview process.

notes: An intensive software engineering program for underrepresented groups in tech. You'll learn mobile and full-stack development while working on projects related to Snapchat. Includes mentorship from Snap engineers.
requirements: Non-traditional background. Basic programming knowledge helpful but not required.

notes: A paid fellowship for underrepresented groups in tech. You'll work on real Spotify features alongside world-class engineers. Great company culture with strong emphasis on autonomy and innovation.
requirements: Early-career engineers from underrepresented backgrounds. Some coding experience required.

notes: A 12-month immersive program placing apprentices on real product design teams across Meta's family of apps. You'll work with senior designers on projects impacting billions of users. Strong mentorship and structured learning path.
requirements: Design portfolio required. Bootcamp grads, self-taught designers, and career changers encouraged. Proficiency in Figma or similar tools.
location: Menlo Park, CA | New York, NY | Remote

notes: Cisco's cybersecurity apprenticeship program trains you in network security, threat detection, and incident response. You earn industry certifications while working on real enterprise security infrastructure. Strong pathway to full-time security roles.
requirements: CompTIA Security+ or equivalent knowledge helpful but not required. Strong interest in cybersecurity and willingness to learn.

notes: Amazon's apprenticeship program places you on real teams building products used by millions. Multiple tracks available including software development, quality assurance, and technical writing. Includes paid training, mentorship, and a pathway to full-time conversion.
requirements: No degree required. Must demonstrate relevant foundational skills. Some tracks require basic coding knowledge.
location: Seattle, WA | Arlington, VA | Multiple US locations

notes: HubSpot's early career program is perfect for breaking into customer success at a top SaaS company. You rotate through different CS functions — onboarding, retention, expansion — while learning HubSpot's platform inside and out. Known for exceptional company culture.
requirements: No tech experience required. Strong communication skills and genuine interest in helping customers succeed.
